packageRules.description: Copier template updates (e.g. momokaya-template) must never automerge — a Copier update can leave unresolved <<<<<<< conflict markers in files (see README), and only a human reviewing the PR diff catches that

Copier stores its `gh:`/`gl:` template-source shorthand verbatim in `_src_path`, but Renovate's git-tags datasource passes packageName straight to `git ls-remote` without expanding it the way Copier itself does — rewrite to the real clone URL so the datasource can resolve tags
oisin-ee shared TS7 + ultracite/oxc toolchain — pin exact versions and open a bump PR for each package as it publishes (no weekly lockstep batch)
Resolve private oisin-ee packages from GitHub tags instead of the npm registry
The TS7 preview (typescript rc + tsgo native-preview) and oxfmt are pre-stable; allow Renovate to offer prerelease/dev bumps for them instead of pinning to a stale stable tag

React family (react, react-dom, react-native) must move together across the fleet — one grouped, pinned PR instead of three independent bumps that can drift out of lockstep. separateMajorMinor:false is required here: that option has priority over packageRule groups (Renovate would otherwise split a major bump into its own PR even inside a group), and a split defeats the fleet-lockstep goal

Effect ecosystem (effect core + every @effect/* subpackage) is fleet-locked as one grouped, pinned PR — separateMajorMinor:false so the group doesn't split into its own major-only PR. ignoreUnstable/respectLatest are opened so the group can pick up Effect's prerelease line the moment a subpackage starts publishing one — checked against the npm registry on 2026-07-08, only the `effect` package itself currently publishes prerelease versions

effect core publishes an explicit npm dist-tag `beta` for the Effect 4 prerelease line (confirmed via the npm registry: dist-tags.beta = 4.0.0-beta.93 vs dist-tags.latest = 3.x) — follow it directly so the group tracks the beta channel instead of falling back to the stable `latest` tag. Scoped to `effect` only: the `@effect/*` subpackages do not carry a `beta` dist-tag yet (verified: zero beta versions published for @effect/platform), and followTag on a package with no matching tag stops all updates for it, not just prerelease ones

Go toolchain — the `go` directive and `toolchain` directive in go.mod both resolve through the golang-version datasource; group them into one PR so the fleet's Go version moves together, with separateMajorMinor:false so a Go major bump can't split off into its own PR. No rangeStrategy is set here: go.mod directives are single exact-version strings with no semver range to pin, so a rangeStrategy would be a no-op
